title: Koeleria gordjaginii
taxonomy: Koeleria gordjaginii is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Koeleria, which comprises several species of grasses. Within the family Poaceae, Koeleria gordjaginii is a member of the subfamily Pooideae. The subfamily Pooideae is a large group of grasses that also includes species like wheat and oats. Koeleria gordjaginii is typically classified as a perennial or annual grass, depending on the specific growing conditions.

intro: Koeleria gordjaginii, also known as Koeler's hair-grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits that include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria gordjaginii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koeleria gordjaginii is often used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as in the form of tinctures and extracts.
